problem.title: Club of Young Aircraft Builders (hard version)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: entries may be fixed or gaps; n,c <= 100, c <= m <= n*c, and total m over tests <= 1e4.
Official editorial page checked as the contest editorial; its tutorial body was lazy-loaded, so the recurrence was cross-checked with public writeups and self-derivation.
Used for the position-aware DP idea: track the boundary of the c-th still-higher value and account for fixed entries before that boundary.
Used for the alternative phase/deferred-contribution DP and how fixed entries restrict transitions and binomial choices.
Used to cross-check the phase DP state: processed prefix, current active floor interval, and count of launches by active floors.
Used to cross-check the small-to-large prefix quota formulation with prefix counts of fixed values.
Re-derived the prefix quota DP from the launch rule and checked how fixed entries block candidate slots.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution index checked; public Luogu article pages above supplied the substantive solution text.
Official editorial submission link; source-code access was blocked by Codeforces browser checking, so it was not used for algorithm details.

annotation.summary: Count completions of a partially fixed length-m launch sequence under the same visibility rule: a launch from floor x is legal only before c previous launches from floors x..n, and the completed day must leave every floor seeing at least c launches.
annotation.constraints: n,c <= 100 and total m <= 1e4; fixed values destroy the symmetry of D1, so the solution needs an O(n*m*c) DP with O(n*m) prefix counts of fixed entries.
annotation.core_idea: A completed sequence must have exactly c copies of n, and each value x is constrained by the total number of smaller values already placed: all x positions must lie within the prefix of length c + count(values < x). Process values from small to large. Let f[x][j] count ways after assigning all values <= x into j positions. For v=x+1, the last allowed position is pos=min(j+c,m). If a fixed v occurs after pos, the state is invalid. Among the first pos positions, j slots are already occupied by smaller values and ge[pos][v] fixed slots are occupied by values >= v, leaving pos-ge[pos][v]-j free gaps. Choose extra occurrences of v among those free gaps by a binomial coefficient, while respecting fixed v entries and forcing exactly c copies when v=n.
annotation.complexity: O(n*m*c) time and O(n*m + m*c) memory, with O(n*m) prefix counts of fixed entries and binomial precomputation.
tricks: turn visibility into the absolute prefix quota c plus the number of smaller values
process values from small to large because each value's allowed prefix depends on smaller counts
use prefix counts of fixed values at least v to remove blocked positions from binomial choices

solution_variants.name: small-to-large-prefix-quota-dp
solution_variants.summary: Process values v=1..n. State j is the number of positions already assigned smaller values. For each possible total count k of v, choose k minus the fixed count of v from free gaps inside min(j+c,m), reject fixed v beyond that prefix, and require k=c for v=n.

solution_variants.name: phase-deferred-contribution-dp
solution_variants.summary: Scan positions left to right with state (current lowest active floor, active visible count). When the active count reaches c, advance the floor layer and multiply by a binomial coefficient after subtracting already fixed positions assigned to that layer.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n*m*c) time, O(n*c) or O(m*c) memory.

tags.evidence: The hard version counts valid completions after assigning value layers under fixed-position constraints.
For each value v the legal positions are cut off by c+count(values<v), and transitions are built around this moving prefix boundary.
After removing fixed and already-assigned positions from the allowed prefix, the transition chooses a subset of remaining gaps for the current value.
The assignment history is compressed to the current value layer and j, the number of positions occupied by smaller values.
Prefix counts of fixed entries at least v compute blocked slots and invalid fixed positions in O(1) per transition group.
The phase DP variant aggregates deferred transitions with prefix sums over previous position states.
